---
title: "FW 536 — Day 2 morning lab (v3 · aquatic & fisheries) — Your Name"
author: "Your Name Here"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    self_contained: true
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 3
    toc_float: true
    code_folding: show
    theme: cosmo
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE)
```

# Day 2 morning lab — Continuous distributions problem set

**Problem set version:** **v3 — aquatic & fisheries theme**.
(The companion version is [v2 — forest & songbird](problem_set_v2.html); you choose whichever theme fits your interests.)

This template matches [`problem_set_v3.html`](problem_set_v3.html). Read each problem statement there, write your work below, **Knit** to HTML, and upload the `.html` to the matching Canvas assignment.

For setup help see the [R Markdown tutorial](../rmarkdown_tutorial.html).

---

# Setup

```{r}
# Load packages you'll need. Add or remove as appropriate.
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(MASS)        # for glm.nb
library(effects)
library(emmeans)
```

---

# Problem 1 — Distribution of fish weights in a creel survey

> You record body weight (g) for every fish kept in a 30-day creel survey on a coastal river. Most fish are sub-legal-size cutthroat (small), with a long right tail of large hatchery steelhead retained in the same fishery. The distribution is strongly right-skewed.

**My work:**

```{r}
# Your R code here
```

**Answer / biological interpretation:**


---

# Problem 2 — Length-weight allometry of Chinook salmon

> For 200 Chinook salmon you have fork length (cm) and weight (g). Lengths range 30–110 cm; weights range 250 g to 18 kg.

**My work:**

```{r}
# Your R code here
```

**Answer / biological interpretation:**


---

# Problem 3 — Juvenile coho counts in snorkel surveys

> From 80 pool surveys, juvenile coho counts are modeled as

**My work:**

```{r}
# Your R code here
```

**Answer / biological interpretation:**


---

# Problem 4 — Smolt survival through a hydro reach vs. temperature

> Acoustic-tagged Chinook smolts tracked through an 80-km reach: outcome is survived (1) or not (0). Predictor is river temperature (°C) at release. Logistic regression returns intercept $+2.20$ and slope $-0.18$ per °C.

**My work:**

```{r}
# Your R code here
```

**Answer / biological interpretation:**


---

# Problem 5 — Pink shrimp catch per tow

> For 120 standard 30-minute trawl tows, shrimp catch has mean 80 individuals and variance 1200.

**My work:**

```{r}
# Your R code here
```

**Answer / biological interpretation:**


---

# (Optional) Embed a photo of hand-written work

For problems easier to do on paper, photograph and embed:

```{r, echo=FALSE, eval=FALSE, out.width="60%", fig.cap="Hand-derivation for Problem N"}
knitr::include_graphics("images/problemN_derivation.jpg")
```

(Change `eval=FALSE` to `eval=TRUE` after saving an image. See the [R Markdown tutorial](../rmarkdown_tutorial.html) for details.)

---

# Submission checklist

- [ ] Answered every morning problem from `problem_set_v3.html`.
- [ ] Knitted to HTML without errors.
- [ ] Photos (if any) embedded with `self_contained: true` in the YAML.
- [ ] Uploaded the `.html` to the matching Canvas assignment.
